cards.entryTitle: Songs, Paintings, and the Feeling of the Highway
cards.title: Western Motel
cards.creator: Edward Hopper
cards.notice: Western Motel shows road travel as glamorous and lonely at the same time, with stillness inside the promise of movement.
cards.wscConnection: Guiding section: Where We're Going, We'll Still Need Them · Big ideas: Home & Wandering / Journeys · Subjects: Visual and Performing Arts / Media / Film

cards.rightsNote: Use exact image; do not generate.

cards.entryTitle: Songs, Paintings, and the Feeling of the Highway
cards.title: Standard Station
cards.creator: Edward Ruscha
cards.notice: Standard Station turns roadside architecture into a bold Pop Art image of speed, fuel, signage, and highway culture.
cards.wscConnection: Guiding section: Where We're Going, We'll Still Need Them · Big ideas: Home & Wandering / Journeys · Subjects: Visual and Performing Arts / Media / Film
cards.imageInstruction: /imagesearch exact image of Standard Station. Use the real artwork/building/place, not a generated substitute. If rights are unclear, use a source link or LINK button rather than embedding.

cards.entryTitle: Songs, Paintings, and the Feeling of the Highway
cards.title: Pearblossom Highway
cards.creator: David Hockney
cards.notice: Pearblossom Highway makes the road feel fragmented, wide, repetitive, and built from many different viewpoints.
cards.wscConnection: Guiding section: Where We're Going, We'll Still Need Them · Big ideas: Home & Wandering / Journeys · Subjects: Visual and Performing Arts / Media / Film

cards.title: Motor hotel / motel architecture
cards.creator: Various architects/developers
cards.notice: Motels were built around cars: parking, roadside access, individual rooms, and quick overnight stops.
cards.wscConnection: Guiding section: Where We're Going, We'll Still Need Them · Big ideas: Home & Wandering / Liminality · Subjects: Design / Architecture / Urbanism / History

cards.title: The world’s first motel / Motel Inn
cards.creator: Arthur Heineman
cards.notice: The world’s first motel / Motel Inn is a motor hotel: a car-friendly lodging form built for parking, short stays, road access, and continued movement.
cards.wscConnection: Guiding section: Where We're Going, We'll Still Need Them · Big ideas: Home & Wandering / Liminality · Subjects: Design / Architecture / Urbanism / History

cards.title: Roadside culture
cards.notice: Gas stations, diners, motels, signs, and small businesses helped turn Route 66 into an experience, not just a road.
cards.wscConnection: Guiding section: Where We're Going, We'll Still Need Them · Big ideas: Journeys / Destinations · Subjects: History / Media / Film

cards.rightsNote: Use real example if available; generated WSC-style visual is acceptable only for concepts.

cards.title: Trackway
cards.notice: A route made visible through repeated movement, showing that roads can emerge before they are formally designed.
cards.wscConnection: Guiding section: Where We're Going, We'll Still Need Them · Big ideas: Routes, Roads & Navigation / Progress · Subjects: History / Design / Architecture / Urbanism

cards.title: Ridgeway
cards.notice: A high route along a ridge, often used because elevated ground can be easier to follow, drier, and more visible.
cards.wscConnection: Guiding section: Where We're Going, We'll Still Need Them · Big ideas: Routes, Roads & Navigation / Progress · Subjects: History / Design / Architecture / Urbanism

cards.title: Holloway
cards.notice: A sunken path worn down by centuries of feet, hooves, wheels, and weather.

cards.title: Gallery road
cards.notice: A road or protected passage built into or along difficult terrain, often showing how roads adapt to mountains or cliffs.

cards.title: Corduroy road
cards.notice: A road made by laying logs across soft or swampy ground so people and vehicles can cross unstable terrain.
cards.wscConnection: Guiding section: Where We're Going, We'll Still Need Them · Big ideas: Routes, Roads & Navigation / Progress · Subjects: History / Design / Architecture / Urbanism

cards.title: Macadam roads
cards.creator: John Loudon McAdam
cards.notice: A road-building method using compacted layers of broken stone, important before modern asphalt became dominant.
cards.wscConnection: Guiding section: Where We're Going, We'll Still Need Them · Big ideas: Routes, Roads & Navigation / Progress · Subjects: History / Design / Architecture / Urbanism
